name: Molly Herb
description: Moly, or Molly Herb, is a mythical plant with magical properties mentioned in Homer's Odyssey. Described as having a black root and a white flower, it was given to Odysseus by Hermes to protect him from the enchantments of the sorceress Circe. Circe used her magic to transform Odysseus's crew into swine, but the moly herb rendered him immune to her powers. The herb's significance lies in its ability to counteract powerful magic and deception, representing resilience, protection, and the triumph of good over evil. It symbolizes the importance of inner strength and divine intervention in overcoming seemingly insurmountable obstacles. The exact nature of the moly herb remains a mystery, fueling speculation about its possible real-world botanical counterpart. Some theories suggest it may have been garlic, rue, or another plant with protective or medicinal properties. Regardless of its true identity, the moly herb has become a potent symbol of resistance against magical threats and a reminder of the power of divine assistance in Greek mythology. Its enduring presence in popular culture highlights its continued relevance as a symbol of protection and magical defense, even in modern narratives. The herb allowed Odysseus to retain his human form and intellect while in Circe's domain, enabling him to free his crew and continue his journey home. Moly's effectiveness emphasizes the vulnerability of even the most powerful heroes to magical attacks and the crucial role of protective measures in navigating the supernatural world.
subtitle: Protection against Circe's magic.

keyMyths.title: Odysseus and Circe
keyMyths.description: Hermes gives Odysseus the moly herb to protect him from Circe's magic, allowing him to resist her attempts to transform him into a pig and ultimately free his crew.

keyMyths.title: Symbol of Resistance
keyMyths.description: The moly herb serves as a symbol of resistance against magical threats and deception, highlighting the importance of divine aid and inner strength in overcoming adversity.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: The primary source describing the moly herb and its use by Odysseus.
